LAW AND MOTION 302, 2 - DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO ONCOLOGY ASSOCIATES MEDICAL GROUP INC.,, KEVIN KIM DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING. OFF CALENDAR FOR FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH MEET-AND-CONFER LAWS. THE GOLDMAN DECLARATION FAILS TO SHOW PARTIES MET AND CONFERRED IN PERSON, BY PHONE, OR BY EMAIL AS TO THE OPERATIVE, FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. (SEE CCP 430.41(A) ["IF AN AMENDED COMPLAINT ... IS FILED, THE RESPONDING PARTY SHALL MEET AND CONFER AGAIN WITH THE PARTY WHO FILED THE AMENDED PLEADING BEFORE FILING A DEMURRER TO THE AMENDED PLEADING."].) RESPONSE TO THE AMENDED COMPLAINT IS NOW DUE BY SEPTEMBER 5, 2025. JUDGE: JOSEPH M. QUINN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (302/JMQ)

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-12-2025 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-27-2025 Continued To Nov-26-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-18-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Timothy Reed, Esq. 669-444-1148 timothyreedesq@gmail.com for Plaintiff.
Christoper Blackburn, Esq. 415-480-0459 cgb@hassard.com Hassard Bonnigton for Defendant. (In-person)

ARGUED. The Court shares views and poses questions to Defense. Mr. Blackburn argues matter should be dismissed. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SUTTER BAY HOSPITALS DBA CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

Defendant Sutter Bay Hospitals' demurrer to the sole cause of action for wrongful death in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Yelena Nechay is sustained with twenty days leave to amend. If she can do so in good faith, plaintiff is granted leave to file a second amended complaint alleging ultimate facts: 1) when and how she discovered Sutter's alleged wrongful conduct, 2) identifying an exact or approximate date within one year of her filing this lawsuit that she made her discovery, and 3) that she could not reasonably have made the discovery on an earlier date. Plaintiff's conclusory allegation that her discovery of the alleged wrongful conduct occurred within the statute of limitations period lacks ultimate facts and does not identify how and when she made that discovery or that she lacked the ability to have made the discovery at an earlier time.

A proposed order is submitted prior to the hearing. Order is signed.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/HEK)

Motion For Terminating Sanctions Or, Alternatively, Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintff Yelena Nechay And Her Counsel; Memorandum In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Aug-07-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Andrew Goldman, Esq. 415-435-5500 for Defendant.
No Appearance of Plaintiff.

RULING - Plaintiff withdraws their notice to contest. Mr. Goldman informs the court sanctions have been received. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs SAN FRANCISCO ONCOLOGY ASSOCIATES MEDICAL GROUP INC.,and KEVIN KIM's Motion For Terminating Sanctions Or, Alternatively, Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintff Yelena Nechay And Her Counsel.

Defendant Kevin B. Kim, M.D. and San Francisco Oncology Associates Medical Group Inc.'s unopposed Motion For Terminating Sanctions Or, Alternatively, Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff Yelena Nechay And Her Counsel is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART.

(see word minutes for full details)

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-05-2025 Continued To Aug-18-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (by Zoom):
Christopher Blackburn, Esq. 415-480-0459 cgb@hassard.comHassard Bonnington LLP for Defendant/Moving party Sutter Bay Hospitals
Dale Ratner, Esq. 415-435-5500 The Goldman Law Firm for Defendant/Moving party San Francico Oncology Assoc/Kevin Kim.

Argued. Mr Ratner argues they met and conferred by phone. Mr. Blackburn cites paragraph 12 on page 3 of a separate declaration confirming the meet and confer occurred. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court vacates the tentative ruling.

1 - DEFENDANT SUTTER BAY HOSPITALS DBA CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

Continued to 8/18/25 at 9am in Dept. 302.

Mr. Blackburn to email a proposed order to contestdept302tr@sftc.org and serve notice of entry of order.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-05-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Christopher Blackburn, Esq. 415-480-0459 cgb@hassard.comHassard Bonnington LLP for Defendant/Moving party Sutter Bay Hospitals
Dale Ratner, Esq. 415-435-5500 The Goldman Law Firm for Defendant/Moving party San Francico Oncology Assoc/Kevin Kim.

Argued. Mr Ratner argues they met and conferred by phone. Mr. Blackburn cites paragraph 12 on page 3 of a separate declaration confirming the meet and confer occurred. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

2 - DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO ONCOLOGY ASSOCIATES MEDICAL GROUP INC.,, KEVIN KIM DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

Off calendar for failure to comply with meet-and-confer laws. The Goldman declaration fails to show parties met and conferred in person, by phone, or by email as to the operative, first amended complaint. (See CCP 430.41(a) ["If an amended complaint ... is filed, the responding party shall meet and confer again with the party who filed the amended pleading before filing a demurrer to the amended pleading."].) Response to the amended complaint is now due by September 5, 2025.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-16-2025 Continued To Sep-24-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories Set One And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-06-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

4-DEFENDANT KEVIN KIM , MD's Motion To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories Set One And For Monetary Sanctions.

Defendant Kevin Kim's Motion to Compel Responses to Special Interrogatories, Set One is GRANTED.

The discovery requests at issue appear reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (CCP 2017.010.) Plaintiff has not established that it has provided any responses to the special interrogatories (SPROGs). No meet and confer requirement exists for motions to compel responses for special interrogatories when no responses have been provided. (CCP Section 2030.290.)

Plaintiff shall serve verified, objection-free, substantive responses to Special Interrogatories Set One by June 30, 2025.

Plaintiff did not act with substantial justification in connection with these discovery requests. Plaintiff and counsel Timothy Reed and Law Offices of Timothy Reed, jointly and severally, shall pay $850 to Defendant Kevin Kim as sanctions, payment no later than June 30, 2025.

Order Submitted.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
